labels.name: postmark-mcp
labels.label: malicious
labels.rationale: Known in-the-wild backdoor disclosed September 2025 — the first publicly-documented malicious MCP server. The send_email tool silently BCC'd every outgoing message to phan@giftshop.club, an attacker-controlled address. The tests/corpus/malicious/postmark_backdoor fixture is modeled on this exact shape. Version pinned to 1.0.16 (the affected release). Note: this version was YANKED from npm after disclosure; `npm pack postmark-mcp@1.0.16` now fails. The harness will surface this as `error` (not FN) until / unless the tarball is preserved out-of-band. Kept in the corpus because the label itself is the most-confident malicious-MCP claim in the public record, and a future re-host of the affected version (or a similar in-the-wild incident) will need the same label format.

labels.name: mcp-server-time
labels.label: benign
labels.rationale: Anthropic-maintained server in the modelcontextprotocol/servers monorepo. Single tool returns current time / converts between timezones. No network, no filesystem, no shell, no env reads. Source has been reviewed by the project authors and is widely installed. confidence at 0.85 (not higher) because MIS itself currently verdicts this `unknown` (the SDK pattern in use isn't detected) — so the label is based on source-reading, not MIS-confirmation.

labels.name: @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything
labels.label: benign
labels.rationale: Anthropic's reference "kitchen sink" server demonstrating every MCP capability — prompts, resources, tools, sampling. Source is the canonical example for the official SDK. Tools include echo, add, longRunningOperation, sampleLLM, getTinyImage; no exfil, no shell-with-input, no secret reads. Widely installed; serves as the SDK test bed. MIS verdicts this `benign` independently (4 of 4 v0.1.7 benign-list entries include it).

labels.name: @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem
labels.label: benign
labels.rationale: Anthropic-maintained server in the modelcontextprotocol/servers monorepo. Exposes filesystem access tools constrained to a configured root directory. The capability is broad by design (read/write/list within the root) but the implementation is a textbook scoped filesystem; no shell, no network, no secret exfil. confidence at 0.80 — the FS-access surface is broad enough that a careless deployment with `/` as the root would expose a lot; benign as shipped, dangerous if misconfigured.

labels.name: mcp-server-fetch
labels.label: benign
labels.rationale: Anthropic-maintained server in the modelcontextprotocol/servers monorepo. The fetch tool retrieves a URL (input-derived host — the legit fetcher pattern) and returns content. No env reads, no shell, no secret exfil. The body uses class-based dispatch which MIS doesn't yet follow (L18), so MIS verdicts this `shallow` — the label is based on source-reading, not MIS-confirmation. confidence 0.85.
